message:
test(ffs-btree): proptest bw_tree materialize/consolidate metamorphic relations

Add two property-based tests for MappingTable, the first proptests in
bw_tree.rs (the existing 132 tests are all unit-style). They pin two
metamorphic relations that the recent fuzz-discovered consolidation
bugs would otherwise be free to violate again silently:

1. proptest_materialize_matches_btreemap_model — for any sequence of
   Insert/Delete ops over a single page, materialize_page must equal
   the BTreeMap produced by replaying the same ops on a model. This
   pins the chain-walk + delta-fold logic in materialize_from_head:
   a regression that re-orders deltas, drops a delete, or applies an
   insert twice diverges from the model on the first such op. Also
   asserts lookup agrees with materialize for every probed key.

2. proptest_consolidate_page_is_idempotent — once a page has been
   consolidated to chain_len=1, calling consolidate_page again must
   not change observable state. Asserts:
     * materialize is preserved across the second call
     * chain_len_before == chain_len_after == 1
     * cas_attempts == 0 (no CAS work on already-consolidated page)

   This is the property whose oracle the fuzz harness was getting
   wrong (entries_count==0 vs the real entries.len()) — pinning it
   in the lib's own test suite makes future drift visible immediately.

64 cases each, all 134 ffs-btree tests pass.
